title: Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple is located in the town of Kothamangalam in Ernakulam district, on the northern bank of Kothayar river, a tributary of Muvattupuzha river.
overview.description: Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple is located in the town of Kothamangalam in Ernakulam district, on the northern bank of Kothayar river, a tributary of Muvattupuzha river. It is believed that this temple is one of the 108 Shiva temples of Kerala and is considered to be the last temple installed by sage Parasurama dedicated to Shiva. Thrikkariyoor (Karuvoor or Karaorai), believed to be the headquarters of Adi Cheras kings, is famous for its historical significance and legends.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thrikkariyoor
topPlaces.description: Thrikkariyoor is a village located in the Nellikuzhi panchayat of Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple is located near the village.

topPlaces.name: Kottappady
topPlaces.description: Kottappady is a village in Kothamangalam Taluk in Ernakulam district of Kerala State, India. It belongs to Central Kerala Division.

topPlaces.name: Kothamangalam, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Kothamangalam ([koːd̪ɐmɐŋgɐlɐm]) is a municipality at the foot of the Western Ghats in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. It is located 42 km (26.1 mi) east of the district collectorate in Thrikkakara and about 218 km (135.5 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kothamangalam
topPlaces.description: Martha Mariyam Cathedral or St. Mary's Church is a prominent Valiyapally (Principal Church) of the Syriac Orthodox Church situated in Kothamangalam town in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India.

image.alt: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kothamangalam

topPlaces.name: Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam
topPlaces.description: Mar Thoma Cheriyapally is a Jacobite Syrian Christian Church Church located in Kothamangalam town of Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. The church is believed to have been established in 1455 by 18 families who separated from Marth Maryam Valiyapally.

topPlaces.name: Nellikuzhi
topPlaces.description: Nellikuzhi is a Grama panchayat in Kothamangalam Taluk, Ernakulam district, Kerala, situated on the Aluva-Munnar State Highway. Nellikuzhi is famous for its furniture business.

topPlaces.name: Venduvazhy
topPlaces.description: Venduvazhy is a village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. The municipal area is Kothamangalam.

topPlaces.name: Cheruvattoor
topPlaces.description: Cheruvattoor is a village in the municipality of Kothamangalam, which is located at the foot of the western Ghats in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (38 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thrikkariyoor Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thrikkariyoor, Kottappady, Kothamangalam, Kerala, St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kothamangalam, Marthoman Cheriyapally, Kothamangalam.
